title: podman rm/stop --cidfile
body:
Add a --cidfile flag to podman rm/stop to pass a container ID via a
file.  Podman run already provides the functionaly to store the ID
in a specified file which we now complete with rm/stop.  This allows
for a better life-cycle management in systemd services.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
What's the intent here? I don't mind the change, but it seems a little strange at first glance

> What's the intent here? I don't mind the change, but it seems a little strange at first glance

That's needed for the _new_ systemd services. `ExecStart` writes the file, `ExecStop` reads it. This way, we don't need to hard-code any IDs or names in the files and make them shareable. I can demo it on Monday.
and I neglected to add you need some bash completions too.
a couple of minor suggestions @vrothberg ... great job and i like it

code LGTM.

Just a question, differently than `create` and `run`, `stop` and `rm` accept multiple IDs.

Could we change `CIDFile` to be a string slice, so that it is possible to do something like?

`podman stop --cidfile /tmp/cid --cidfile /tmp/cid2 ...`

> Could we change `CIDFile` to be a string slice, so that it is possible to do something like?
> 
> `podman stop --cidfile /tmp/cid --cidfile /tmp/cid2 ...`

Sure, I can extend the PR to cover this case as well, both for `rm` and `stop`.

Updated:
* `--cidfile` can now be specified multiple times. Tests have been updated accordingly.
* Added bash-completions.
* Updated man pages.
* s/checkAllAndLatest/checkAllLatestAndCIDFile/
